rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the implementation scope when binding implementation-level signal routings.
Preserve AADL structure by ensuring all referenced features and connections exist in the input model and are not invented.
generic_rules: Implementation-level contracts must reference only subcomponents and connections that are explicitly declared in the AADL model.
Component type and implementation scope boundaries must be respected: type scope for interface contracts, implementation scope for internal routing bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed as `annex agree {** ... **};` and placed within correct AADL component or implementation blocks.
Only output features or declared auxiliary variables may appear on the left-hand side of assignments in implementation annexes.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) or event ports as numeric or Boolean values in AGREE expressions.
Avoid using subcomponent port paths in component type annexes — use only declared features or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
